title: ecumenical councils
author: joshualinog
body:
# overview - Οικουμενικές Σύνοδοι

## etymology
from [Ancient Greek](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ancient_Greek_language) [οἰκουμένη](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%CE%BF%E1%BC%B0%CE%BA%CE%BF%CF%85%CE%BC%CE%AD%CE%BD%CE%B7#Ancient_Greek) (oikouménē) 'the inhabited world') denoted the known, inhabited, or habitable world.

The [Greek](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ancient_Greek) term cited above is the [feminine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grammatical_gender) [present](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Present_tense) [middle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Middle_voice) [participle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Participle) of the verb οἰκέω (oikéō, '(I) inhabit') and is a [clipped form](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clipping_(morphology)) of οἰκουμένη γῆ (oikouménē gē, 'inhabited world').[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ecumene#cite_note-oed-1)[[2]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ecumene#cite_note-2)

# overview - Οικουμενικές Σύνοδοι

## etymology
from [Ancient Greek](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ancient_Greek_language) [οἰκουμένη](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%CE%BF%E1%BC%B0%CE%BA%CE%BF%CF%85%CE%BC%CE%AD%CE%BD%CE%B7#Ancient_Greek) (oikouménē) 'the inhabited world') denoted the known, inhabited, or habitable world.

The [Greek](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ancient_Greek) term cited above is the [feminine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grammatical_gender) [present](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Present_tense) [middle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Middle_voice) [participle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Participle) of the verb οἰκέω (oikéō, '(I) inhabit') and is a [clipped form](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clipping_(morphology)) of οἰκουμένη γῆ (oikouménē gē, 'inhabited world').[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ecumene#cite_note-oed-1)[[2]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ecumene#cite_note-2)
